name: CreditClaw
description: Self-hosted cards for AI agents with spending controls. Bot owners fund agents with allowances and guardrails. Daily/weekly/monthly budgets. Wallets and x402 coming next.
notes: OpenClaw-native. Stripe-powered. Targets individual bot owners.

name: Proxy
description: Virtual cards and payment infrastructure for AI agents. Dedicated card per agent/workflow with hard spending limits enforced at network level. Attestation-before-access model. MCP server integration.
notes: Published comprehensive agent payments landscape report. Strong MCP integration story.

name: Crossmint
description: Full agentic payments platform: agent wallets (fiat + stablecoin), virtual cards via Visa Intelligent Platform, agentic checkout API for Amazon/Shopify, agent credentials (W3C). Merchant of Record for returns/chargebacks.
notes: Visa Intelligent Platform partner. Also does stablecoin wallets, x402, and credential issuance. Very broad offering.
positioning: Developers and enterprises building agentic commerce apps

name: Skyfire
description: Agent identity and payments network. KYA (Know Your Agent) protocol with signed JWTs for agent identity verification. Agent Checkout protocol for autonomous account signup, login, and payment. Digital wallets with spending limits.
funding: Backed by a16z CSX, Coinbase Ventures
usage_data: Visa pilot partner. Apify integration live.
notes: Focus on agent identity layer. KYA protocol is unique differentiator. Powers Consumer Reports agent demo.
positioning: Agent platforms and developers needing identity + payments

name: Nekuda
description: Agentic commerce infrastructure using 'Mandate Model'. Captures user intent (what agent can buy, spending limits, approval rules) and stores/retrieves payment credentials just-in-time. Visa Intelligent Commerce launch partner.
funding: $5M from Madrona, Amex Ventures, Visa Ventures (May 2025)
usage_data: Pilots with Gensmo, Price.com, Henry Labs
notes: Strong VC backing from strategic investors. '20 lines of code' integration pitch.

name: PayOS
description: Network-issued Agent Tokens for AI-driven transactions at any card-accepting merchant. Processor-agnostic. Integrates with both Visa and Mastercard networks. First live Mastercard Agentic Token transaction (Sept 2025).
usage_data: First live MC Agentic Token transaction. BeyondStyle/Jomashop pilot.
notes: Token-based approach vs card-based. Broad merchant acceptance but shared credential risk.
positioning: Developers and agent platforms needing network-level card rails

name: Prava
description: Payments API for AI agents. Prepaid wallets separate from bank accounts/credit cards. One developer wallet funds multiple agents with individual spending rules. Tokenized card payments. Visa Intelligent Commerce integration.
notes: Multi-agent wallet architecture. Good for platforms running many agents simultaneously.

name: Visa Intelligent Commerce
description: Visa's platform enabling AI agents to transact on behalf of cardholders. Includes Trusted Agent Protocol (TAP) for cryptographic agent identity, network tokenization, and partner ecosystem (Skyfire, Nekuda, PayOS, Crossmint).
usage_data: Hundreds of secure agent-initiated transactions completed (Dec 2025). Predicts millions of consumers by 2026 holiday.
notes: The network-level enabler. TAP launched Oct 2025.

name: Mastercard Agent Pay
description: Mastercard's agentic AI payments platform. Rolled out to all U.S. cardholders by Nov 2025. Enables secure, scalable AI agent-initiated purchases on Mastercard rails.
notes: Broad rollout. PayOS first to demo live MC Agentic Token. March 5, 2026: launched Verifiable Intent, an open-source cryptographic audit trail framework linking consumer identity, agent instructions, and transaction outcome into tamper-resistant records. Uses Selective Disclosure for privacy. Interoperates with Google UCP/AP2. March 4, 2026: first live agentic transaction in Singapore with DBS and UOB (ride booking via hoppa/CardInfoLink). Also completed first live end-to-end AI agent payment in Europe with Santander (March 2026). Expanding APAC with regional AI Centre of Excellence in Singapore. Live in Australia, New Zealand, India as well. March 11, 2026: launched Mastercard Agent Suite, an advisory/consulting offering (4,000+ advisors) helping businesses build, test, and deploy custom AI agents. Available Q2 2026. Focus on product discovery for banks and conversational shopping for merchants. Complements Agent Pay.

name: MoonPay Agents
description: Non-custodial infrastructure layer enabling AI agents to create wallets, fund them, and transact in digital assets autonomously. Built on MoonPay CLI. Supports recurring purchases, cross-chain swaps, x402 compatibility, and fiat off-ramps. Human completes one-time KYC, then agent operates within permissions.
notes: Crypto-native on-ramp for agents. Non-custodial wallets stored on user device. x402 compatible. Bridges fiat-to-crypto for agent economy.

name: Stripe Shared Payment Tokens (SPTs)
description: Payment primitive for agentic commerce. Lets AI agents initiate payments with a customer's permission and preferred payment method without exposing underlying credentials. Supports Visa agentic network tokens, Mastercard Agent Pay tokens, and BNPL (Klarna, Affirm). First and only provider supporting both agentic network tokens and BNPL tokens through a single primitive.
usage_data: Adopted by Etsy, URBN (Anthropologie, Free People, Urban Outfitters). Klarna + Affirm BNPL integration launched March 3, 2026.
notes: Launched Oct 2025. Major expansion March 3, 2026: added Visa + Mastercard agentic network tokens and BNPL (Klarna, Affirm). Positions Stripe as the unified aggregator across all agentic payment rails.
positioning: Merchants and platforms accepting agent payments

name: Google Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP)
description: Open standard for agentic commerce enabling AI agents to complete purchases on behalf of consumers across Google AI Mode and Gemini. Standardizes the programmatic exchange of information (via API, MCP, or A2A) between AI agents and merchant backends. Supports native checkout (direct integration) and embedded checkout (iframe). Merchant remains Merchant of Record. Open-source spec on GitHub.
usage_data: Developed with Shopify, Etsy, Wayfair, Target, Walmart. Splitit, dozens of retail partners endorsing. Open-source: github.com/Universal-Commerce-Protocol/ucp
notes: Announced January 2026. Help docs and merchant onboarding published March 2026. Roadmap includes multi-item carts, account linking for loyalty, post-purchase tracking/returns. Positions Google as the agentic commerce surface layer (discovery + checkout) while merchants keep customer data. Significant because it bridges AI agent surfaces directly to merchant checkout, complementing card-level solutions from Visa/MC/Stripe.
positioning: Merchants selling through Google AI surfaces

name: AgentCard
description: Prepaid virtual Visa cards for AI agents. Instant issuance via CLI (npm install -g agent-cards). Single-use by default, locked to loaded amount. MCP integration for Claude Code, Cursor, and any MCP runtime. Card data (PAN, CVV) encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM, decrypted only on explicit request. Works anywhere Visa is accepted.
notes: Developer-first: two CLI commands to issue a card. Single-use cards limit blast radius per task. Native MCP integration (agent-cards setup-mcp). Stripe-powered checkout. Founded by @keyserfaty.
positioning: Developers / Claude Code and Cursor users

name: J.P. Morgan Payments + Mirakl Nexus
description: Enterprise agentic commerce platform combining Mirakl's Nexus (AI-optimized product catalogs, agent discovery, pricing/inventory access) with J.P. Morgan Payments' infrastructure (secure payment processing, tokenization for agent transactions, fraud protection). Enables AI agents to discover, evaluate, and purchase products autonomously on JPM's payment rails. Verified agent identity, user-controlled permissions, bank-grade risk management.
usage_data: Working with select retailers and merchants in closed beta.
notes: Announced March 10, 2026. First major bank building dedicated agent payment infrastructure. Mirakl is a French commerce software firm (marketplaces, dropship). JPM's Mike Lozanoff (global head of merchant services) leading. Positions JPM as the bank-grade payment layer for agentic commerce, complementing network-level solutions from Visa/MC.

name: Ramp Agent Cards
description: Corporate cards built for AI agents from Ramp, the leading corporate spend management platform. Agents get real spending ability governed by hard spend limits, merchant controls, and full transaction visibility. Credentials generated via API, MCP, or CLI. Built on Visa Trusted Agent Protocol (Visa Intelligent Commerce).
notes: Announced March 11, 2026. Ramp is one of the largest corporate card/spend management platforms. Fits into Ramp's broader agentic stack (AP Agents launched Oct 2025). Flexible integration: API, MCP, and CLI for credential generation. Uses Visa Intelligent Commerce / Trusted Agent Protocol. Significant because it brings agent cards to Ramp's massive existing enterprise customer base.
positioning: Businesses already on Ramp / corporate spend management

name: Privacy.com
description: Virtual cards with card-level spending controls for AI agents. Spending limits (daily/monthly/annual), merchant-locked cards, single-use cards, category-locked cards (e.g. travel only). Controls enforced at card authorization level before transaction is approved. Stablecoin-funded cards (USDC) coming soon.
notes: Consumer-friendly positioning vs developer-first competitors. Works anywhere cards are accepted, no merchant integration needed. Published OpenClaw-specific setup guide. Stablecoin-funded cards (USDC) on roadmap, bridging crypto and traditional rails.
positioning: Consumers and prosumers / anyone giving agents spending power
